description: Structural parity validation of generated BenchFlow task directories from OpaqueToolsBench BFCL base configs

checks.name: required_files_present
checks.description: task.toml, instruction.md, Dockerfile, test.sh, evaluate.py, ground_truth.json all exist and are non-empty

checks.name: instruction_md_valid
checks.description: instruction.md contains ## Query, ## Available Functions, and output path reference

checks.name: ground_truth_valid
checks.description: ground_truth.json is valid JSON with non-empty ground_truth key

checks.name: test_sh_executable
checks.description: test.sh has executable permission bit set

checks.name: dockerfile_valid
checks.description: Dockerfile contains FROM directive and /logs/verifier directory creation

eval_parity.description: End-to-end evaluation parity: build Docker image, run evaluate.py with correct/wrong/missing agent responses, verify binary rewards.
eval_parity.notes: AST-based deterministic matching. All test cases produce correct binary rewards (1.0/0.0). No LLM judge variance.

security_parity.description: Verify that adversarial agent payloads cannot execute arbitrary code via eval() in the verifier. After removing eval() fallbacks in parse_python_call(), ast.unparse() is used instead.
security_parity.fix_applied: Replaced eval(compile(ast.Expression(...), '', 'eval')) with ast.unparse() at both keyword-arg and positional-arg locations in parse_python_call()
security_parity.notes: ast.unparse() returns a safe string representation without executing the AST node. Adversarial payloads are treated as non-matching function names and receive reward 0.0.
